content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges provided, we can scan each edge and collect the time t whenever node u and node v match the two queried nodes (in either order).

First, we will find the earliest time when node 5 and node 2 are connected. We will use a queue to keep track of the nodes we have visited and the earliest time we have found so far.

Initially, we add both nodes 5 and 2 to the queue and dequeue them. We set the earliest time to 0.

Next, we iterate through the edges of the graph:

* For the edge (2, 4, 0), we add node 4 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.
* For the edge (3, 6, 0), we add node 3 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.

* For the edge (3, 4, 7), we add node 4 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.
* For the edge (0, 3, 8), we add node 8 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.
* For the edge (0, 5, 9), we add node 9 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.

* For the edge (1, 2, 12), we add node 2 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.
* For the edge (2, 7, 12), we add node 7 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.
* For the edge (2, 9, 13), we add node 9 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.
* For the edge (0, 1, 14), we add node 1 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.

* For the edge (0, 2, 19), we add node 2 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.
* For the edge (5, 8, 19), we add node 8 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.
* For the edge (6, 8, 19), we add node 6 to the queue and dequeue it. We update the earliest time to the minimum of the current earliest time and the time of the dequeued node.

Now we have the following earliest times:

* 0: 0
* 1: 1
